title: Stage 3 claim debug dumps persist decrypted seed phrases on the claim artifact lane
boundary_slice: operator, admin, and debug-only surface slice
protected_asset: wallet seed phrase and decrypted secret confidentiality
trust_boundary: feature-gated wallet debug export -> durable claim artifact lane
attacker_capability_model: a malicious operator, CI artifact collector, or same-user reader of a feature-enabled simulator run can recover durable seed phrases from generated claim outputs
main_vulnerability: Stage 3 writes export_wallet_debug_{name}.json into the normal outputs/claim lane, and those JSON files include decrypted DebugSecretEntry rows with plaintext_b64 and seed_phrase, so the live debug-export path bypasses the private-lane constraint enforced only for the Stage 2 markdown artifact.
implementation_nuance: The simulator normalizes wallet_debug_dump in Phase 047 release-style validation flows, publicly reaches debug_export_wallet from z00z_wallets, and only verifies Stage 3 dump existence. The private-lane README and test controls cover Stage 2 markdown output, not the Stage 3 claim JSON sink.

evidence.summary: wallet_debug_tools is documented as exporting decrypted .wlt contents and line 171 aliases it through wallet_debug_dump for simulator usage
DebugSecretEntry exposes plaintext_b64, plaintext_utf8, and seed_phrase fields
debug export derives a seed_phrase for SECRETS_SEED_MAIN and persists decrypted plaintext fields into the debug entry
Stage 3 writes export_wallet_debug_{name}.json under out_claim and line 312 feeds that path into debug_export_wallet
Stage 3 finalize logs outputs/{claim_dir}/export_wallet_debug_*.json and confirms the files were written
live generated claim output contains a full seed_phrase, with plaintext_b64 on the same path at line 46304
guard tests keep Stage 3 debug exports behind wallet_debug_dump but do not require a private path or redaction

pros: exact production-code path exists from the wallet debug helper into Stage 3 simulator output generation
live repository artifacts already prove the claim-lane JSON dump contains seed phrases and plaintext blobs
the current README and tests promise a narrower private lane than the live Stage 3 sink actually uses
cons: the attacker needs a feature-enabled debug-capable build or access to artifacts from such a run
private-permission writes reduce exposure to same-user or artifact-reading contexts rather than world-readable disclosure

verification.reason: candidate exceeded the skeptical gate with production-code reachability, a realistic operator or artifact-reader model, partial existing controls, and live non-test artifact proof

defenses: move Stage 3 debug JSON outputs off the normal claim lane or remove DebugWalletDump.secrets from the serialized artifact
add regression coverage proving Stage 3 claim outputs never contain seed_phrase or plaintext_b64
upgrade Stage 3 verification from file-existence checks to redaction and private-path assertions
fail closed when wallet_debug_dump is enabled with a non-private claim output path
residual_risk: Historical repository, local, or CI artifacts may already contain durable seed phrases and decrypted secret bytes until those outputs are scrubbed and any non-simulated secrets are rotated.
